question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
note: He names a concrete growth blocker — being pulled into execution details despite wanting bigger scope — which signals strong growth constraint with some self-awareness about the gap.

question.label: Execution details
question.name: Execution details — where are you still getting pulled in most often?
question.description: Drills into the specific work pulling Ahmed back into execution so the blocker becomes actionable.

answer: When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
note: Retreating into direct delivery when conversations get political signals a strong growth blocker and a mild clarity/alignment gap around how to operate through influence.

question.label: Political conversations
question.name: Political conversations — what makes those moments hardest for you to stay in?
question.description: Drills into the specific dynamic that makes Ahmed retreat from influence into execution.

answer: I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
note: He names a specific gap in senior-level work — hesitation around cross-org alignment — which signals a strong growth gap and mild role/influence clarity weakness. [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'cross-org alignment' is a concrete thread, but drill cap requires advancing stages.

question.label: Stretch now
question.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?
question.description: Distinguishes comfort-zone execution from genuine growth moments.

answer: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
note: He names low confidence in senior forums as the stretch point, signaling a real growth edge with some hesitation to lean into it.

question.label: Senior forums
question.name: Senior forums — what about those rooms makes you feel unprepared?
question.description: Drills into the specific confidence thread to surface the real gap underneath his hesitation.

answer: I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
note: He names a self-limiting pattern — not being explicit about desired opportunities — which signals stalled growth and mildly reduced agency.

question.label: Wanted opportunities
question.name: What opportunities have you not been explicit about wanting?
question.description: Turns his self-identified pattern into a concrete aspiration he can name clearly.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
note: He names a concrete growth aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is strong positive growth signal; wellbeing is mildly positive through forward-looking energy.

question.label: Direction
question.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?
question.description: Probes whether they have a real picture of where they want to go.

answer: I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
note: He names delegation as a needed change to make room for strategic ownership, which shows forward intent but also that the future state is not yet in place; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] 'delegate more of the hands-on work' could be picked up next session.

question.label: Tradeoff ask
question.name: What would you stop doing if you wanted to spend more time on strategic ownership?
question.description: Forces a real tradeoff between tactical delivery and senior-level work, making the cost of change explicit.

answer: A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
note: Names a missing structure — a clear growth contract with milestones — which signals low clarity on the path forward and mild growth friction.

question.label: First move
question.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to start first?
question.description: Closes by turning the conversation into one concrete choice Ahmed will make before the next check-in.

answer: I want to own one strategic bet end-to-end and set a milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.
note: He named a concrete next move — owning one strategic bet end-to-end — which is a positive growth commitment signal.
